name: Strait of Hormuz
summary: Day 52 Sunday evening. INSTITUTIONAL CODIFICATION INTACT, KINETIC IN PAUSE, GULF MATRIX HARDENING ON UAE LEG. Saturday SNSC ratification of IRGC framework remains operative (transit certificates, $1/bbl loaded-tanker fees, military + hostile vessels denied). No further IRGC kinetic Sunday — Saturday's burst (Jag Arnav VLCC, Sanmar Herald, container-ship projectile, Mein Schiff 4 cruise threat) is one event so far, not a cadence. CENTCOM's Sunday posture update repeats the 23-vessel blockade figure with no new kinetic claim. Sadjadpour 72-hour test tracking toward 'warning-shot-for-leverage thesis holds' at 36-hour mark. Sunday evening NEW: (1) UAE ADNOC chairman Al Jaber publicly: 'Strait not open, needs to be open unconditionally' — first UAE public hardening (single-chain pending Monday corroboration); (2) Iranian FM Araghchi Sunday X posts reframe chokepoint effect as insurance-market problem rather than IRGC action ('insurers fear the war of choice you initiated — not Iran') — operationally correct given Lloyd's AWRP ~1% Friday and VLCC TD3C WS 513 day-rates, but it sets up Tehran's civilian wing to claim 'open in principle' while harvesting SNSC fee revenue. Sunday Globex Brent indicatively ~$98 (+8.4% over Friday wire-ICE $90.38) confirms paper-physical convergence by paper rising to physical (Dated ~$132 Thursday). Kpler tape ~2 tankers since April 8 ceasefire; ~1-2% pre-war baseline. Gulf media: UAE hardening, Saudi mediating, Qatar neutral, Oman silent — four-way Gulf posture favours diplomatic optionality.

signals.content: IRGC announced Hormuz closed to US, Israeli, and Western allied ships. Shipping traffic reduced to a trickle — 21 tankers total since Feb 28 vs 100+/day pre-war (CNBC tracker data).
Dallas Federal Reserve described closure as 'largest disruption to energy supply since the 1970s energy crisis' and 'largest in the history of the global oil market.'
Mojtaba Khamenei's first statement confirmed Hormuz would remain closed to pressure Iran's enemies.
Insurance companies cancelling war risk coverage for vessels in Gulf. 150 ships stranded around the strait. Marine war risk insurance prices rising 50-100%.
Trump issued 48-hour ultimatum for traffic to resume, threatened to strike Iran's power plants. Hormuz closure entering 4th week with no resolution.

IRGC Navy chief Tangsiri killed at Bandar Abbas — the operational HQ for Hormuz closure. Impact on operations unclear: could disrupt IRGC naval coordination or harden resolve. (Sources: IDF, CENTCOM, Al Jazeera)

IRGC formalized tolled passage through Iranian territorial waters around Larak Island. Approved vessels receive code and IRGC escort. Yuan-denominated payment at ~$2M/transit. 142 transits March 1-25 vs 2,652 baseline = 95% reduction. Transforms blockade from military closure to controlled monetized chokepoint. (Sources: USNI News, Fortune, Lloyd's List Intelligence, NBC News)

No change in Hormuz transit data from March 27 snapshot. IRGC toll booth system continues operating — yuan-denominated payments, escorted passage around Larak Island. 142 transits March 1-25 vs 2,652 baseline (95% reduction). CENTCOM claims 92% of IRGC Navy's largest vessels destroyed but toll operations unaffected. Tangsiri assassination (March 26) has not visibly disrupted IRGC naval enforcement.

Pentagon preparing 'weeks of limited ground operations' including Kharg Island raids (Al Jazeera). Trump to FT: 'Maybe we take Kharg Island, maybe we don't.' 82nd Airborne + 2 MEUs deploying alongside USS Tripoli Marines. Brent pulled back to $107.71 (-4.3%) on diplomacy hopes — but ground ops prep suggests Hormuz military escalation simultaneously. Toll system continues operating. (Sources: Al Jazeera, Axios, CNN, FT)

EVENING UPDATE: Trump claimed (Times of Israel) that Iran agreed to let 'another 20 boats' through Hormuz as 'sign of respect,' beginning Monday. UNVERIFIED — Iran has NOT confirmed. Unclear whether incremental to existing toll system (~142 transits Mar 1-25) or Trump's recharacterization of existing traffic. Trump also told FT: 'maybe we take Kharg Island' — reiterating oil seizure rhetoric. IEA: 'closure of the Strait of Hormuz is the biggest oil shock in history.' Toll system continues. (Sources: Times of Israel, FT, IEA via CNBC)

Iran's selective Hormuz opening EXPANDING: FM Araghchi announced ships from China, Russia, India, Iraq, Pakistan allowed (March 26). Malaysia and Thailand subsequently added. Pakistan secured specific deal for 20 Pakistani-flagged vessels (2/day) — announced by Pakistan FM Dar. Strategy: split the coalition by rewarding neutrals while maintaining blockade against US/Israel/Western allies. IRGC toll booth system (yuan-denominated, $2M/transit, Larak Island escort) continues. 142 transits March 1-25 vs 2,652 baseline = 95% reduction PERSISTS despite selective opening. Trump's '20 boats' claim appears to conflate Pakistan deal with broader data. Trump's 'obliterate' post explicitly conditions infrastructure destruction on Hormuz not being 'immediately open for business.' April 6 deadline hardening.

EVENING UPDATE — PARADIGM SHIFT: WSJ reported Trump told aides he's willing to end military campaign even if Hormuz remains largely shut. Leavitt: Hormuz reopening 'not a core objective.' This BREAKS the market's assumed resolution path (war ends → Hormuz reopens → oil normalizes). Pakistan 20-ship deal EXECUTING — MV Multan (general cargo) and P-Akili (80M+ litres crude) crossed Hormuz, expected at Karachi March 31. Japan in negotiations — Araghchi says Iran 'prepared to allow.' Two Chinese-owned ultra-large containerships DENIED per Windward — individual vetting applies. Iran's parliament drafting toll legislation to formalize Hormuz authority — potentially making selective control PERMANENT regardless of ceasefire. IRGC toll continues: yuan-denominated, $2M/transit, Larak Island escort. Fewer than 10 commercial vessels/day vs normal 20-30.

WSJ paradigm shift CORROBORATED by CNN: 'Trump administration believes it will have to end Iran war with oil chokepoint still closed.' Leavitt called Hormuz reopening 'not a core objective.' Market pricing confirmation: Brent surged ~5% to ~$118.60 on April 1 DESPITE equities rallying on peace hopes — oil and equities moved in SAME direction. Oil up on structural supply concern, equities up on peace. The market is telling us: even if war ends, Hormuz disruption persists. Iran's parliament drafting toll legislation to formalize permanent selective control. IEA 400M barrel SPR release flowing at 1.0-1.5M bpd but US SPR drops to 243M barrels — lowest since 1984 — failing to contain prices. (Sources: WSJ, CNN, Bloomberg, DOE)

Al-Salmi VLCC attacked at Dubai port anchorage — 31 nautical miles NW of Dubai, OUTSIDE Hormuz proper. Demonstrates Iran willing to strike in allied Gulf waters, not just at chokepoint. War risk insurance must now reprice for entire Gulf, not just Hormuz transit. Concurrent: Greek container ship off Ras Tanura hit, Thai cargo ship near Hormuz (3 crew missing). Day 33 zero Houthi Red Sea shipping attacks — commercial closure via carrier withdrawal (Maersk, CMA CGM, Hapag-Lloyd, MSC rerouted), not military blockade. (Sources: Bloomberg, UKMTO, Maritime Executive, gCaptain)

CONFIRMED: Hormuz toll law passed FULL Majlis (not just committee). Codifies Tehran's 'sovereignty, control and oversight' over the Strait, establishes transit fees payable in Iranian rials, bans US/Israel-linked vessels and countries that joined unilateral sanctions. Still needs Guardian Council review + presidential signature. De facto ~$2M/vessel. GCC Secretary-General condemned — fees violate UNCLOS. US Naval War College: 'no legal basis.' GCC: 'will not accept bilateral arrangements.' This formalizes the WSJ/CNN Hormuz decoupling paradigm — Iran legislating permanent selective control regardless of ceasefire outcome. (Sources: Turkiye Today, Anadolu, CNN, Bloomberg)

CONFIRMED: AQUA 1 tanker struck 17nm north of Ras Laffan in Qatar waters — 3 cruise missiles fired, 2 intercepted, 1 hit. Demonstrates Iran's ability to project force across Gulf waters, not just in the Strait itself. Attack pattern expanding: Hormuz chokepoint → Dubai port (Al-Salmi VLCC) → Qatar waters (AQUA 1). Combined with toll law, Iran establishing de facto maritime control over entire Arabian Gulf. (Sources: QatarEnergy, Al Jazeera, AGBI)

CONFIRMED: War risk insurance at 1%+ hull value ($10-14M/voyage), 5x pre-crisis levels. Lloyd's JWC redesignated entire Arabian Gulf as conflict zone. Insurance premium IS the market's probability assessment — 'commercial risk logic has become an irregular warfare tool at Hormuz' per Irregular Warfare Journal. Hormuz tanker traffic collapsed 80%+. Commercial closure achieved through insurance costs making transit economically unviable, not through total military blockade. (Sources: Lloyd's List, S&P Global, Irregular Warfare Journal)

CONFIRMED: ~40 VLCCs anchored outside Yanbu with 5-day waits, 64 ships en route. Diverted Hormuz traffic is crushing Saudi Red Sea port capacity. Commercial closure is accomplished via carrier withdrawal (Maersk, CMA CGM, Hapag-Lloyd, MSC rerouted), not military blockade. Some transit continues through IRGC toll system but at 80%+ reduction from baseline. (Sources: maritime data, Bloomberg)

Iran's Majlis reportedly passed Hormuz toll law — codifying sovereignty, transit fees in rials, banning US/Israel/sanctions-allied vessels. Requires Guardian Council review + presidential signature (neither confirmed). GCC condemned as UNCLOS violation. This LEGISLATES the WSJ/CNN Hormuz decoupling paradigm — permanent selective control regardless of ceasefire. De facto tolls ~$2M/vessel via IRGC escort continue. 95% reduction persists. April 6 energy pause 3 days away. (Sources: Turkiye Today, Anadolu, CNN, Bloomberg)

April 6 deadline HOURS away. Trump 48-hour ultimatum: 'Open the f***in' Strait' or face destruction of power plants and bridges. Iran rejected: 'gates of hell.' Zero extension signaled. Zero diplomatic progress. Toll law still awaiting Guardian Council review. IRGC toll system continues (~$2M/transit, yuan-denominated). 95% reduction persists. If deadline expires without extension, US resumes energy infrastructure strikes. (Sources: Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, Fortune, Newsweek)

CONFIRMED: Deadline EXTENDED 20 hours to Tuesday April 7, 8 PM ET. Iran's IRGC responded with permanent closure threat: 'The Strait of Hormuz will be completely closed and will not be opened until our destroyed power plants are rebuilt.' This exceeds prior rhetoric — linking Hormuz reopening to RECONSTRUCTION, not just ceasefire. Separately, Oman-Iran Hormuz transit talks REPORTED (The National, Abu Dhabi) — Oman and Iran discussing 'measures for smooth transit,' potentially a parallel track for partial reopening short of full capitulation to Trump's demands. 45-day ceasefire framework (Axios) includes Hormuz as one of Iran's two primary bargaining chips (alongside HEU stockpile). Toll law still awaiting Guardian Council review. IRGC toll system continues. 95% reduction persists.

Bloomberg Hormuz Tracker: weekly transits hit highest since war began — 21 ships over April 5-6 weekend (13 outbound, 8 inbound). Iraqi crude tanker transited after Iran granted 'brotherly Iraq' exemption. Eight Indian LPG tankers now through. First Western-flagged vessel (French-owned) permitted April 3. Permitted nations: China, Russia, India, Iraq, Pakistan, Malaysia, Thailand, Philippines, select Western on case-by-case. HOWEVER 21/weekend is still ~1/7 of pre-war daily 138. IRGC 'New Persian Gulf Order' classifies countries as hostile/neutral/friendly — selective opening widening but tightly controlled. IRGC Navy: Hormuz will 'never return to its former state.' Iran's 10-clause ceasefire counter-proposal includes Hormuz protocol (not unconditional reopening). Tuesday deadline is TODAY. (Sources: Bloomberg Hormuz Tracker, Energy Connects, Seatrade Maritime, Al Jazeera, PressTV)

BLOOMBERG HORMUZ TRACKER: WEEKLY TRANSITS REACH WAR HIGH [CONFIRMED]. The seven-day rolling average for Hormuz transits has reached the highest level since the war began. Iran's three-tier system (hostile/neutral/friendly) is quietly working — vessels flagged to China, Russia, India, Iraq, Pakistan, Malaysia, Thailand, and the Philippines are transiting. Two Qatar LNG tankers were quietly allowed through under a Pakistan-brokered secret deal. Iraqi-linked oil tanker exited Hormuz via Iran on April 5. The 'closure' Trump is using as casus belli is now factually a partial selective opening — though crucially, it remains closed to Western/hostile-flagged vessels and OPEC+ exports cannot increase. Trump's 'Strait closed' pretext is partially fictional. Iran's strategy is winning quietly. Bloomberg's data is sourced from Vortexa and Kpler ship-tracking — independently verifiable.

IRAN'S 10-POINT COUNTER demands recognition of Iranian sovereignty over Hormuz + 'safe-passage protocol' (Iranian regulation, not free reopening). Trump's 'open the Strait or we destroy your power plants' demand is now partially met. Iran's position: 'It IS open — to friends. We will not open it to enemies.' The semantic gap is the war's fulcrum. If Trump accepts the partial reopening as 'compliance,' the deadline can lapse. If he insists on Western/Israeli access, escalation is locked in.

EVENING UPDATE: WTI-Brent inversion CONFIRMED — WTI $114.53 > Brent $110.80 ($4.60 premium). Market reclassifying Gulf crude as 'semi-stranded.' WTI Midland offered at $34 premium over Dubai benchmark (OilPrice.com). On like-for-like basis June Brent still above June WTI ($107.95 vs $96.36) — headline inversion partly contract-month mismatch, but underlying signal real. OPEC+ 206K bpd for May conditional on Hormuz reopening (Bloomberg) — paper decision, cannot be delivered. JPMorgan warned $150+ if Hormuz stays shut into mid-May. Iran's 10-point counter includes Hormuz 'safe passage protocol' — $2M transit fee split with Oman, revenue to reconstruction. Carnegie's Sadjadpour: Iran 'openly talking about formalizing Hormuz control as if it's going to be their own Panama Canal.' (Sources: CNBC, Bloomberg, OilPrice.com, JPMorgan, i24NEWS)

CEASEFIRE PROMISES 'COMPLETE OPENING' (TRUMP) vs 'CONTROLLED PASSAGE' (IRAN) — STRUCTURAL CONTRADICTION [CONFIRMED]. Trump's announcement of the 14-day ceasefire conditioned the suspension on Iran agreeing to a 'COMPLETE, IMMEDIATE, and SAFE OPENING' of the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's FM Araghchi specified the deal includes 'safe passage' through the Strait 'via coordination with Iran's Armed Forces' — the same selective-control framework Iran has maintained since the IRGC toll system was established March 27. This is NOT 'complete opening' in the Western sense — it is Iranian sovereignty over the Strait dressed as ceasefire compliance. The semantic gap is the war's structural fulcrum and the most likely point of failure when Friday April 10 Islamabad talks begin. Markets repriced as if 'complete opening' was real — Brent -13.5%. Lloyd's: physical normalisation cannot be compressed by political will; reinsurance restoration is sequential, multi-party, and takes weeks even on a holding ceasefire.

Iran RE-CLOSED the Strait of Hormuz on April 8-9 citing Israeli strikes on Lebanon as a ceasefire violation. Bloomberg ship-tracking data shows only 3-4 ships transited April 8-9, down from 11 on Tuesday and 9 on Monday. First post-ceasefire transits were 2 bulk carriers (Greek-owned NJ Earth and Liberia-flagged Daytona Beach) — NOT oil tankers. 479 vessels remain stranded including 426 tankers, 34 LPG carriers, 19 LNG ships per MarineTraffic. Pre-war throughput ~100-120 vessels/day; current ~4-5/day = 95%+ reduction persists. White House Press Secretary Leavitt called re-closure 'false'; Defense Secretary Hegseth: 'The strait is open' — both contradicted by Bloomberg operational data. CBS News timeline: even if ceasefire holds perfectly, oil exports return to previous levels by July 2026 at earliest.

War risk insurance rates dipped post-ceasefire to 0.35-0.45% of vessel value (from peak 3.5-10%), but remain 7-10x pre-war levels. For a $150M LNG carrier, still adds ~$500K-$675K per voyage. Neil Roberts (LMA): 'It is highly unlikely that trade into the Gulf will simply resume.' Reinsurers exercised 72-hour cancellation clauses at war start; restoring coverage takes weeks, not days. Lloyd's Market Association confirmed 88% of participants still have appetite to underwrite hull war risks.

Day 43. THREE CHINESE VLCCs (Cospearl Lake, He Rong Hai, Yuan Hua Hu) halted at strait, NOT through — ceasefire failed its first operational test. IRGC toll system remains in force. S&P Global: ~9 vessels transited April 8-10 combined vs 100+/day pre-war = ~3% capacity. Stranded vessel count revised UP: Bloomberg 800+, IMO 2,000, Press TV/Global Security 3,000 with 94% reduction. 20,000+ seafarers trapped. NBC News: 'effectively at a standstill despite ceasefire.' CNBC: 'may take months to recover.' War risk insurance at 1.5-3.0% hull value. LMA Neil Roberts: safety, not insurance, is the bottleneck. Russia-China vetoed Bahrain UNSC Hormuz resolution — closing multilateral reopening pathway. (Source: Bloomberg AIS, NBC News, CNBC, S&P Global, UN News)

Day 44. Traffic DECLINING post-ceasefire: 11 ships Apr 8 (including 9 tankers), 5 ships Apr 9 (ZERO oil/gas tankers per Kpler), 6-7 ships Apr 10 (2 tankers, 3 cargo, 1 bunkering). Three Chinese VLCCs (Cospearl Lake, He Rong Hai, Yuan Hua Hu) still NOT through. Russian VLCC MT Arhimeda (ex-Vizuri, US-sanctioned) transited INBOUND to Kharg — testing inbound passage. ADNOC CEO Sultan Al Jaber: 'The Strait of Hormuz is not open. Access is being restricted, conditioned and controlled. That is not freedom of navigation. That is coercion.' Windward AI estimates ~3,200 vessels stranded west of Hormuz (including ~800 tankers) — higher than Bloomberg 800+ or 479 subset tracker. IRGC published navigational map with alternative corridors to avoid mines — FIRST official Iranian acknowledgment mines are deployed. Creates PERMANENT operational justification for escorted passage: even if political leaders agree to 'free passage,' IRGC can argue ships need escort through mined waters. Oman formally rejected IRGC toll proposal citing UNCLOS (undersecretary-level meeting April 4 accepted monitoring protocol but rejected toll component). No nation has formally accepted the toll protocol. IRGC tiered pricing confirmed: allies (China, Russia) $0.50-0.70/bbl; neutral (India) $0.80-1.00; high-risk (EU, Japan, South Korea) $1.20-1.50; hostile (US, Israel) prohibited. Payment via yuan (Kunlun Bank/CIPS), Bitcoin, or USDT — entirely outside SWIFT. Kpler analyst Ana Subasic: 'Safe transit capacity expected constrained max 10-15 passages daily' even under best conditions. White House NEC Director Hassett admitted traffic 'about 10% of normal pace' — still overstating operational data (5-7/day vs 120-140 normal = ~4-5%).

Day 45. US Navy mine clearing operation launched — first US warship transit since war began. Two Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyers USS Frank E. Peterson (DDG-121) and USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112) entered the strait on April 11 while Islamabad talks were underway ~2,000 miles away. CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper stated: 'Today, we began the process of establishing a new passage, and we will share this safe pathway with the maritime industry soon to encourage the free flow of commerce.' Additional underwater drones will join clearance in coming days. CRITICALLY: accounts DIVERGE on what happened. CENTCOM and US officials (via WSJ, Axios) say both ships completed full transit. Iran's state TV reported IRGC Navy issued 30-minute warning to USS Michael Murphy threatening attack if it continued, and that the vessel 'turned back.' A regional intelligence official (cited by Bloomberg) said both destroyers were 'forced to turn back after encountering threats from Iran's IRGC, which also launched a UAV in the direction of the vessels.' Iran called the crossing a ceasefire violation. IRGC threatened 'any attempt by military vessels to pass through the Strait of Hormuz will be dealt with severely.' Trump claimed 'all 28 of Iran's mine dropper boats are lying at the bottom of the sea' (unverified). The truth matters: if the US successfully transited, it breaks IRGC's physical control narrative; if they turned back, IRGC control is reinforced even against the US Navy. This represents a UNILATERAL US approach — if mine clearing succeeds and a safe commercial corridor is established, it creates an alternative to Iran's IRGC toll system that does not require Iranian consent.

AFTERNOON UPDATE -- TRANSIT EVIDENCE TILTS TOWARD US VERSION: Two critical data points. (1) Fortune/WSJ report USS Michael Murphy deliberately activated AIS on far side of strait -- unusual move that signals successful transit ("purposeful messaging about having sailed through" per expert). (2) Iran's denial has sharpened: Zolfaghari told state TV the CENTCOM claim is "strongly denied"; IRGC Navy: "full authority to manage the Strait of Hormuz intelligently." WSJ-obtained radio recording from civilian ship captured IRGC saying "This is the last warning. This is the last warning" during transit. IRGC reportedly launched drone toward vessels. Assessment: AIS activation on far side is operational data -- if ships had not transited, IRGC would not need to issue last warning. Iran's denial is face-saving. McNally (Rapidan Energy) projects US could degrade IRGC maritime interdiction to "manageable levels" by late April with underwater drones and littoral combat ships arriving in coming days -- potentially enabling commercial escort operations without Iranian consent. Trump "out-blockade" rhetoric (Truth Social) frames Hormuz confrontation as US initiative. (Sources: Fortune/WSJ, CENTCOM, Zolfaghari via IRIB, IRGC Navy via IRIB, McNally/Rapidan via Fortune, Trump Truth Social via Jerusalem Post)

US naval blockade of Iranian ports begins — CENTCOM confirmed implementation April 13 at 10:00 AM ET. Applies to all maritime traffic to/from Iranian ports, enforced impartially. Preserves non-Iranian Hormuz transit. Mine clearing (Phase 1, April 11) escalated to blockade (Phase 2). AIS evidence from April 11 favored US completing initial transit (USS Michael Murphy transponder activated on far side of strait). IRGC: blockade is ceasefire violation, military vessels will face 'severe' response. Iran spokesperson 'strongly denied' US transit was completed. War risk insurance expected to re-spike from ceasefire rates (0.35-0.45%) back toward 1.5-3%. (Sources: CENTCOM, CNBC, Bloomberg, Fortune, WSJ, Al Jazeera)

US blockade Day 1 operational results: Comoros-registered sanctioned tanker M/T Elpis — part of Iran's shadow fleet — successfully transited Hormuz AFTER the blockade took effect without US interception, directly testing blockade credibility. M/T Ostria (Botswana-flagged, landlocked = false registry) changed destination 41 minutes after deadline. M/T Rich Starry (Malawi-flagged, also landlocked) broadcast 'drifting' off Qeshm Island then initially reversed — BUT see evening correction below. Total traffic 31 vessels in 24 hours with 6 carrying cargo, versus normal 80-100/day. Blockade functioning primarily through deterrence rather than kinetic interception. (Sources: Bloomberg, CBS News, MarineTraffic via Tempo.co, Fortune, The War Zone — 5+ independent sources)

EVENING CORRECTION: Rich Starry (Malawi-flagged, Chinese-owned, ~250K bbl methanol from Hamriyah) initially reversed near Qeshm Island but made SECOND attempt in evening and appears to have passed through Strait by early Tuesday UTC. Al Jazeera reports 'at least three vessels, including two US-sanctioned tankers' entered the Gulf on Day 1. Combined with Elpis transit, blockade credibility is weaker than morning assessed — US is NOT interdicting sanctioned/shadow-fleet vessels, only deterring legitimate commercial shipping. Perverse outcome: Iran's illicit oil network continues while legitimate trade is strangled. (Sources: Bloomberg, Nikkei Asia, Al Jazeera, Lloyd's List — all April 14)

EVENING CORRECTION on war risk insurance: Lloyd's List clarifies P&I clubs have NOT cancelled war risk cover. Coverage IS available but at prohibitive prices — 2.5-5% of hull value ($10-14M per VLCC trip). LMA: 88% of Lloyd's marine war market participants 'continue to have appetite to underwrite.' The constraint is PRICE not AVAILABILITY. De facto barrier for most commercial operators even though market exists. A diplomatic breakthrough could normalize premiums in weeks (not months) since insurers have capacity. US DFC $40B reinsurance facility attempting to fill the pricing gap. (Sources: Lloyd's List, CNBC, Kennedys Law, Caixin — 4 independent sources, April 14 evening)

UK and France announced joint independent multinational Hormuz mission — Macron and Starmer will co-host conference 'in coming days' for a 'peaceful multinational mission' to restore freedom of navigation, described as 'strictly defensive' and 'separate from the belligerents.' UK explicitly refused to join US blockade — PM Starmer announced British military assets will NOT block Iranian ports. This creates a THIRD PATHWAY between US blockade approach and IRGC control. If coalition materializes, it could provide insurance cover and military escort framework enabling commercial shipping without requiring Iran's permission or US blockade success. (Sources: CNBC, Bloomberg, Times of Israel, Middle East Eye, Outlook India — 5 independent sources)

Saudi Arabia's East-West oil pipeline restored to full capacity (~7 million bpd), diverting oil to the Red Sea port of Yanbu — a critical workaround for the Hormuz blockade. This reduces the blockade's impact on total Gulf oil supply reaching global markets, though Yanbu port capacity constraints remain. (Sources: Al Jazeera, CS Monitor — 2 independent sources)

IRGC fast-attack craft 60% intact despite six weeks of US/Israeli strikes, reported by WSJ. These small, fast vessels operate from underground pens along rocky coasts and evade satellite detection. Trump acknowledged on Truth Social that the US 'did not consider them much of a threat' but then threatened to 'ELIMINATE' any approaching ships. Their intact status means the risk of a US-IRGC naval incident at the blockade line remains operationally present. (Source: WSJ — single source, REPORTED)

Ceasefire (announced April 8, expiring ~April 22) nominally holds at the bilateral Iran-US level — no kinetic breach by either side on blockade Day 1. However, Iranian-backed Iraqi militias continue drone attacks on Gulf states despite ceasefire — Bahrain intercepted seven drones and summoned Iraq's charge d'affaires. Iran considers the blockade a ceasefire violation; the US considers it enforcement within the ceasefire framework. 8 days remain before expiry. (Sources: PBS, CBS News, ISW — 3 independent sources)

Blockade Day 2-3: CENTCOM Commander Admiral Cooper claimed blockade 'completely halted economic trade going into and out of Iran by sea in less than 36 hours' — DISPUTED by independent shipping data. Al Jazeera (Kpler): 3+ vessels including 2 US-sanctioned tankers entered Gulf on Day 1. Panama-flagged Peace Gulf heading to Hamriyah (UAE, typically moving Iranian naphtha) not heading to Iranian port — exploiting blockade's narrow legal scope. Blockade deterring LEGITIMATE commercial shipping while Iran's shadow fleet continues — perverse outcome. UK-France announced independent multinational Hormuz mission conference April 18 in Paris, 40+ countries including Netherlands, Germany, Italy, Canada, Australia, UAE. Macron: 'peaceful, multinational, strictly defensive' — creating THIRD PATHWAY between US blockade and Iranian control. Ceasefire 6-7 days to expiry (April 21-22). Neither side has declared ceasefire ended despite blockade. (Sources: CENTCOM, Al Jazeera/Kpler, Bloomberg, Macron statement, Republic World/ANI, Euronews, NPR, CNBC)

CONFIRMED: UK-France virtual Hormuz conference scheduled for Friday April 17 from Paris with ~40 countries confirmed (France, UK, Germany, Netherlands, Italy, Canada, Australia, UAE). UK leading diplomatic track; France handling military coordination — 'assessing available assets from participating nations and how they could be deployed operationally' (Foreign Policy). CRITICAL SCOPE NARROWING: France emphasized naval escort mission 'would only take place when the war is over,' stated mission is 'restoring freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz WHEN SECURITY CONDITIONS ALLOW' (Tempo, Middle East Monitor). This is a POST-WAR insurance/protection framework, NOT active escort capability that would immediately normalize insurance premiums. Market's 'Friday produces operational escort' bull case appears unavailable on this timeline. Still matters — commitment to defensive post-war framework is itself insurance-market signal. Iran IRGC threatened 'any military vessels approaching the Strait will be dealt with harshly' (Fars News April 14-15); Iran's London ambassador said Tehran is 'considering' strikes on British bases. (Sources: Middle East Monitor, Tempo, Foreign Policy, Tribune India, Athens Times, Global Banking and Finance, Euronews, Fars News)

Blockade Day 4: CENTCOM Admiral Brad Cooper hardened claim — 10 vessels turned around, 'ZERO ships broken through.' Military deployment: 12+ warships (Abraham Lincoln CSG, Tripoli LHA, multiple DDGs), 100+ aircraft, 10,000+ personnel. Legal Insurrection/HotAir (Republican-aligned) amplify 'fully implemented, halting 90% of Iran's sea trade.' CNN counter-framing: 'So why are some ships going through Strait of Hormuz?' — noting the Strait transit vs. Iranian port distinction. CENTCOM 'ZERO' claim either (a) covers only post-Day 1 activity, (b) counts only ships actually heading to Iranian ports (distinct from Strait transit), or (c) overstates. Iran's counter-blockade (April 13) remains DECLARATORY — no confirmed IRGC kinetic enforcement against vessels bound for UAE/Saudi/Kuwait/Qatar ports after 4 days. Maximal-rhetoric/minimal-execution pattern continues. Kpler/Vortexa Day 3-4 shadow-fleet transit data not yet published (typical 24-48hr lag). War risk insurance STICKY at 0.8-1.0% hull = insurers NOT stepping down on Trump interview; require signed framework. Frontline +3.9% April 16 = shipping specialists disagreeing with deal thesis. (Sources: CENTCOM, Al Jazeera, CNN, NBC News, Military Times, Lloyd's List, Strauss Center)

Saudi East-West pipeline restored to full 7 million bpd capacity confirmed April 12 — fastest major operationally significant supply-side repair of the war. Iranian drone attack April 9 on pumping station had reduced throughput by 700K bpd. Pipeline had been converted to full 7M bpd capacity on March 11. Yanbu port bottleneck remains the BINDING constraint: 68% of VLCCs experienced >36-hour anchorage delays in March; port nominal capacity 4-4.5M bpd vs 7M bpd pipeline inflow. Pipeline restoration means more oil reaches Red Sea than any point since blockade began — but physical-futures gap ($132 Dated Brent vs $95 futures) signals market pricing port bottleneck as binding constraint, not pipeline. Iran has NOT claimed follow-up strike, suggesting IRGC preserving ceasefire window through Round 2. (Sources: Al Jazeera April 12, The National, Wikipedia East-West pipeline, Fortune March 28, Houseofsaud)

Day 50 / Blockade Day 5-6. JCS CHAIRMAN CAINE CLARIFIED: BLOCKADE COVERS PORTS AND COASTLINE, NOT THE STRAIT [CONFIRMED — official statement]. This is the single most important definitional shift — it resolves the apparent contradiction between CENTCOM's 'no ships broke through' and Kpler/Al Jazeera tracking 14 vessels transiting the Strait since Apr 13 (including 2 Lloyd's Shadow Fleet, 1 OFAC-sanctioned, 2 Iran-linked). The Strait is technically OPEN; Iran's PORTS are blockaded. Pentagon updated deterrence count to 13 vessels as of Apr 16 (up from 6 Day 1, 10 Day 2). Iran released video of 'near-engagement' — IRGC Navy vs US vessel, no shots fired, but first visual evidence of proximity confrontation. Iran proposed allowing ships through Omani side of Hormuz without risk (Reuters) — US no formal response. (Sources: JCS Caine via official statement, CENTCOM, CNN, CNBC, Reuters, Fortune)

SHALAMAR TRANSIT [REPORTED — single CNN source, no independent AIS/MarineTraffic confirmation]. CNN April 17 liveblog reported Pakistani-flagged Shalamar, carrying ~440,000 barrels of crude bound for Karachi, transited Hormuz Friday. Vessel 'periodically disabled its transponder for security reasons' — standard shadow-fleet practice. Additive to Pentagon's '14 vessels transited' count since April 13 and to Caine's 'port blockade not strait closure' clarification. Pakistan as flag state is politically notable: Sharif shuttle + Munir Tehran visit position Pakistan as Iran's preferred mediator; Shalamar moves while South Korea's 26 vessels remain stuck. Political access to Iran is a transit-permission variable, not just flag neutrality. Tag REPORTED — do NOT let single-source datum become plural 'Pakistani tankers now transiting.' (Sources: CNN April 17 liveblog)

MACRON-STARMER VIRTUAL SUMMIT FACT-CHECK: 30-40 NATIONS VIRTUAL, NOT 70-80 IN-PERSON [CONFIRMED]. Starmer arrived Paris April 17 to co-host what multiple outlets describe as a VIRTUAL meeting of the Strait of Hormuz Maritime Freedom of Navigation Initiative. CNN: '40-nation virtual summit'; ABC News/clickondetroit: 'about 30 countries including some from the Middle East and Asia.' Macron said mission 'strictly defensive in nature,' limited to non-belligerent countries, deployed 'when security conditions allow' (post-war, not during blockade). Participants contribute 'each according to their capabilities.' No communiqué detailing force composition, rules of engagement, command structure, or timeline published. US excluded as 'warring party.' Of Middle East/Gulf states only UAE and Bahrain attending; Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, Pakistan absent — consistent with region's preference for Pakistan-led diplomatic track over Europe-led security track. South Korea's 26 detained vessels (173 crew) remain stuck; Blue House 'do our best' language unchanged. Summit's practical utility as commercial-escort framework LOW near-term; post-ceasefire multilateral backstop utility HIGHER. Material downgrade in perceived diplomatic weight vs morning brief's 70-80 in-person implication. (Sources: CNN April 17 liveblog, ABC News/clickondetroit, Bloomberg pre-summit, Al-Monitor)

Blockade Day 5 — IRGC ZERO kinetic action continues. CENTCOM 13 vessels turned around count unchanged. No shots fired, no IRGC-US engagement beyond Thursday's 'near-engagement video' (Fortune). UANI April Iran Tanker Tracker confirms ~25 tankers loaded crude at Kharg Island in April; as of April 16 satellite imagery identifies 7 VLCCs, 1 Suezmax, 2 Aframax in Kharg region, with 3 actively loading (~5M bbl new cargo). Shalamar transit additive to this count. 'Port blockade not strait closure' structural distinction continues to define operational reality. War-risk insurance remains at 0.8-1.0% hull value with 35-50% no-claims bonus applied — unchanged from April 13 baseline. (Sources: CENTCOM, UANI April Iran Tanker Tracker, JMIC/UKMTO)

HORMUZ 'COMPLETELY OPEN' DECLARATION + TASNIM REBUKE + IRGC PERMISSION REGIME [CONFIRMED declaration with OPERATIONAL CONDITIONALITY]. FM Araghchi on X April 17 (~17:00 EST): 'In line with the ceasefire in Lebanon, the passage for all commercial vessels through Strait of Hormuz is declared completely open for the remaining period of ceasefire.' Triggered Brent -7.6% to $91.87, WTI -11.3% to $84.00, Heating Oil -12.8%, S&P record high 7,126.06. Tasnim rebuked the tweet as 'flawed and incomplete'; Baghaei clarified conditions: passage via Iran-designated 'coordinated route' only, AND 'if US blockade of Iran ports continues, Iran will take reciprocal measures.' IRGC Navy then issued formal permission regime — 'all ships require its permission to pass, with civilian vessels allowed only through designated Iranian routes, while military vessels remain barred.' Operational data: Kpler 8 vessels transited by 2pm EST Friday (~10-15% of 120/day pre-war); oil-tanker traffic still near-zero. CENTCOM 21 vessels turned back since April 13 (up from 13). Trump reiterated 'blockade in full force until peace deal.' Two distinct 'openings' in play: Iran claims Hormuz open to commercial vessels, US says Iranian port blockade intact. Iranian oil tankers still cannot move. Trigger point 4 (Iranian selective control) REINFORCED — permission regime IS the operational codification of the controlled-passage paradigm. (Sources: Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, NBC News, Argus Media, Kpler via DNYuz, Tasnim, Iran MFA via Tribune India/ANI, Trump via CNN, CENTCOM)

PHYSICAL-FUTURES GAP WIDENS TO ~$40 — widest of crisis. Dated Brent physical still ~$132 (Thursday last-print, no public Friday print located) while Brent futures crashed to $91.87. Gap widened from ~$36 (evening brief April 17) to ~$40. Paper market priced aggressive de-escalation; physical tanker pricing did not. VLCC TD3C rates ~$130K/day (WS ~78) with war-risk surcharge intact; Gulf AWRP ~1% hull + 35-50% NCB UNCHANGED from April 13 baseline. This is the cleanest single-metric read that the futures market and the physical market are pricing fundamentally different paths. If Monday Islamabad delivers, physical should converge down to $100-105 over 5-10 sessions; if reciprocal-measures trigger fires, futures snap back to $95+. One price is materially wrong. (Sources: yfinance BZ=F, Argus/Platts absent Friday print, Baltic Exchange/Clarksons, Lloyd's List/Strauss Center)

SNSC FORMAL RATIFICATION — INSTITUTIONAL CODIFICATION OF IRGC CLOSURE [CONFIRMED — 4 independent sources]. Iran's Supreme National Security Council (civilian apex, chaired by Pezeshkian) issued evening statement read on IRIB late Saturday IST declaring Iran 'determined to enforce monitoring and control over transit through Strait of Hormuz until the definitive end of the war and the establishment of lasting peace.' Statement operationalises Saturday IRGC framework: (1) full vessel information collection, (2) 'transit certificates' per Iran's wartime regulations, (3) fees of $1/bbl on loaded tankers (empty free) for 'security, safety, and environmental protection services.' Military and 'hostile' (US-flagged, Israeli-linked) vessels explicitly denied passage. SNSC frames US blockade as 'ceasefire violation' and declares Araghchi's Friday 'conditional and limited reopening' suspended until US lifts blockade. ICG framing: 'textbook escalation ladder — converts rhetorical tool into priced instrument, allows Iran to claim revenue while claiming sovereignty.' This is the 'Panama-Canal-ification' Sadjadpour warned of (April 7), now institutionally endorsed by civilian-apex. TASS endorsement as 'legitimate Iranian sovereignty' + Xinhua factual endorsement as 'sovereign waters' means Russia and China would veto US-led UN resolution. (Sources: Press TV, Tasnim, Washington Examiner, Al Jazeera live-blog)

KINETIC BROADENS — CONTAINER SHIP HIT BY PROJECTILE + CRUISE LINER VHF-THREATENED [REPORTED, kinetic CONFIRMED via UKMTO chain]. Beyond Saturday morning IRGC fast-boat fire on VLCC Jag Arnav (Indian-flagged, ~2M bbl Iraqi crude) and nearby Sanmar Herald: (1) UKMTO and UK Ministry of Defence separately reported an unidentified container ship was damaged by 'unknown projectile' in the same Strait approach Saturday, with visible container damage (Military Times, Al Jazeera citing UKMTO). Operationally distinct from small-arms gunfire — a projectile (rocket/missile/drone) widens escalation ladder from warning-shot to standoff munition. (2) Malta-flagged cruise liner Mein Schiff 4 reported 'nearby splash' off Oman coast; master documented IRGC VHF threat to 'fire at and destroy the ship' per UKMTO advisory traffic. Passenger-vessel threat expands target set from commercial cargo to passenger shipping — if it escalates would trigger IMO-level maritime emergency, not just commercial insurance response. Target breadth: tanker → container → passenger within 24 hours is a SWEEPING PATTERN, not a single warning shot. Single-chain sourcing (UKMTO) — photographic corroboration not yet public for cruise threat. (Sources: UKMTO Advisory traffic, Military Times, Al Jazeera live-blog, Wikipedia 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis timeline for corroboration)

INDIA MEA SUMMONS IRANIAN AMBASSADOR FATHALI [CONFIRMED — 4 independent sources]. India's Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri summoned Iran's Ambassador Saturday evening IST, conveying 'deep concern' on the Jag Arnav + Sanmar Herald shootings and urging Iran to 'resume at the earliest the process of facilitating India-bound ships.' First formal India-Iran diplomatic friction of the 52-day crisis; India previously maintained strategic-autonomy silence through every prior escalation. Framing deliberately narrow: 'deep concern' (not condemnation), urge resume facilitation (not stop harassment) — preserves off-ramp, but diplomatic ratchet is unprecedented. Substantive because India is Iran's largest current oil customer in the soon-expiring waiver window (Jaya 2M bbl, Felicity 2M bbl discharged; Derya + Lenore rejected Saturday) and Indian-flagged tonnage creates a direct MEA-enforceable legal nexus. (Sources: The Wire, The Tribune, NewsX, The Federal)

CENTCOM NON-RESPONSE AT 16-18 HOURS [CONFIRMED non-event]. As of 09:00 IST Sunday, approximately 16-18 hours after UKMTO/UK MoD report of IRGC gunboats firing on Jag Arnav and the container-ship projectile incident, CENTCOM has issued no public statement specifically corroborating or responding to Saturday's kinetic. Only CENTCOM-direct materials in April 17-19 public record: (i) April 17 release of AH-64 Apache patrol imagery over Hormuz (pre-kinetic, defensive posturing tied to blockade), (ii) pre-existing Admiral Brad Cooper blockade statement — standing-posture line, not re-issued with Saturday's incidents as trigger. The '2+ independent operational sources' bar for upgrading the 'Iran executes reciprocal measures' trigger to active remains UNMET by strict reading — UKMTO and UK MoD are one sourcing chain; CENTCOM and Kpler/MarineTraffic imagery of specific incidents have not surfaced. Three plausible readings: (a) CENTCOM holding response for Monday when Washington opens; (b) deliberately not escalating via press because Apache imagery is the deterrence signal; (c) quiet back-channel activation under way. Trump's 'productive conversations' line supports (c); 'drop bombs again' line supports neither. Administration has not briefed any outlet first — deliberate silence is itself intelligence. (Sources: CENTCOM official account, Pentagon briefing room, The Aviationist, Washington Examiner/USNI News)

GREGORY BREW (EURASIA GROUP) ANALYST ENDORSEMENT OF 'EFFECTIVE CONTROL' THESIS [REPORTED]. Eurasia Group energy analyst Gregory Brew (reliable, conservative base-case track record on Iran flows) told GZERO Media and CNBC weekend that 'Iran now holds effective control over the critical oil chokepoint' and as long as dueling blockades stand 'few — if any — ships will get through the Strait of Hormuz' — US gasoline could approach or exceed $5/gallon by summer. Consistent with Kpler data (~2 oil/gas transits since April 8 ceasefire, ~1-2% of pre-war baseline). Brew's falsifiable prediction: multi-week ~0 throughput while SNSC language stands. A non-Iran, non-Israeli Western analyst with a pro-market lens now endorsing what April 18 brief called 'the normalisation narrative never matched the data.' (Sources: GZERO Media 'The Debrief', CNBC)

ARAGHCHI SUNDAY X POSTS REFRAME CHOKEPOINT EFFECT AS INSURANCE-MARKET PROBLEM [CONFIRMED — RHETORIC]. FM Araghchi Sunday April 19: 'Strait of Hormuz is not closed. Ships hesitate because insurers fear the war of choice you initiated — not Iran.' Significance: Iran's civilian wing now explicitly naming the insurance market — not the IRGC kinetic — as the operational chokepoint mechanism. Operationally, this is correct (Lloyd's AWRP ~1% Friday, expected step to 1.2-1.8% Monday; VLCC TD3C WS 513 with $130-150K/day earnings vs ~WS 50 nominal) but it sets up a separate Iranian framing where Tehran can claim 'open in principle' while harvesting fee revenue under SNSC's $1/bbl regime. The civilian-IRGC factional gap is narrative-only, not operational.

NO FURTHER IRGC KINETIC SUNDAY — SATURDAY'S BURST IS ONE EVENT, NOT A CADENCE [CONFIRMED non-event via UKMTO, CENTCOM, Reuters, CNN, Times of Israel]. Through ~20:00 IST Sunday April 19, no additional ship fired on, no new UKMTO advisory beyond Saturday's 038-26 on container-ship projectile, CENTCOM's Sunday posture update repeats the 23-vessel blockade figure without new kinetic claim, no IMO-level cruise-ship incident corroborated beyond master-report on Mein Schiff 4. MarineTraffic AIS shows handful of vessels still transiting (Iranian-flag skewed, AIS spoofing per USNI). Sadjadpour's 72-hour falsifiable test (fresh fire through Tuesday IST = ladder stepped up; quiet = warning-shot-for-leverage thesis holds) tracking toward 'thesis holds' branch at the ~36-hour mark. CEILING signal for Monday Brent: $100+ would have required Sunday-kinetic repeat.

UAE ADNOC CHAIRMAN AL JABER — 'STRAIT OF HORMUZ IS NOT OPEN AND NEEDS TO BE OPEN UNCONDITIONALLY' [REPORTED single-chain pending Monday corroboration — DIPLOMATIC SIGNAL]. Sultan Al Jaber, ADNOC chairman and former COP28 president, declared Sunday April 19 that the Strait is 'not open' and must be opened 'unconditionally.' Material hardening from morning brief's 'conspicuously quiet' UAE posture. Gulf diplomatic matrix Sunday evening: Saudi FM Faisal mediator-adjacent (3 calls with Araghchi Apr 9-14), Qatar neutral broker, Oman silent, UAE now publicly hardening on the chokepoint issue. Timing — Sunday before waiver expiry and before any Islamabad Round 2 — suggests UAE pre-positioning a public tough line rather than playing quiet convenor. The National (Abu Dhabi) carries Al Jaber's framing with editorial support; UAE information space aligned against Iran on chokepoint rights. Single-chain as of Sunday evening; await Al Monitor / The National corroboration Monday.

edges.context: Day 52. SNSC FORMAL RATIFICATION April 18 — civilian apex body institutionalises IRGC permission regime. Transit certificates + $1/bbl fees on loaded tankers (empty free). Military and 'hostile' vessels denied passage. Araghchi's Friday 'completely open' framework SUSPENDED until US lifts port blockade. Civilian-IRGC-Supreme-Leader three-way split operationally collapsed. Edge transformed from conditional-reopening to institutional codification: Iran's civilian apex is now operationalising a priced chokepoint as a strategic instrument — Panama-Canal paradigm. Kpler ~2 oil/gas tanker transits since April 8 ceasefire (~1-2% of pre-war ~120/day).

Day 51. Friday April 17 was a full trading session (corrects prior brief's Good-Friday error — Good Friday 2026 was April 3). Araghchi Hormuz-open declaration drove Brent -7.6% to $91.87 (wire $90.38, -9.1%), intraday low $87.94. WTI -11.3% to $84.00; spread compressed to $7.87 (yfinance) — still 2-3x historical $2-5. Physical-futures gap WIDENS to ~$40 (Dated Brent ~$132 held vs Brent futures $91) — widest of war. Paper priced aggressive de-escalation; physical did not follow. Goldman Q2 $90 base case validated within $2; Morgan Stanley $110 Q2 now ~$20 above market.

Major LNG exports from Qatar transit or are affected by Hormuz. Qatar halted Ras Laffan LNG production. AQUA 1 tanker struck near Ras Laffan. European TTF at EUR 47.51/MWh. EU storage at 30% (five-year seasonal low). Toll law + Gulf-wide attacks make LNG resumption impossible even with ceasefire.

Day 52. Kinetic broadened Saturday from tanker-only to multi-type (container ship projectile hit + cruise liner VHF-threatened within 24 hours). Target set widened from commercial cargo to passenger shipping. Frontline +5.6% Friday (physical-market signal). VLCC TD3C ~WS 513 (moderated from WS 600+ peak); ~$130-150K/day earnings. War-risk ~1% hull unchanged Friday but insurers did NOT step down on Saturday kinetic — Monday AWRP re-hike likely to 1.2-1.8%. SNSC institutional codification means Sunday intel forces physical-futures gap to converge upward; velocity is the question.

Day 52. Indian-flagged tonnage directly fired-upon (VLCC Jag Arnav ~2M bbl Iraqi crude, Sanmar Herald bridge-window damage). India MEA summoned Iran Ambassador Fathali Saturday evening — first formal India-Iran friction of crisis. Waiver T-0 Monday 05:30 IST; Bessent 'no renewal.' India has received ~4M bbl Iranian crude in waiver window (Jaya + Felicity discharged; Derya + Lenore rejected). 50% of India's crude transits Hormuz; 91% LPG from Gulf. Diplomatic ratchet unprecedented but narrowly framed ('deep concern' not condemnation; urges resume facilitation not stop harassment) — preserves off-ramp.
Iraq's southern oil exports depend on Hormuz. Iraq declared force majeure on all foreign-operated oilfields (Mar 20), cut production from 3.3M to 900K bpd. 90%+ of Iraq's income from oil.

Day 49. Saudi East-West pipeline restored to full 7M bpd capacity April 12 after April 9 Iranian drone attack cost 700K bpd — fastest major repair of war. Yanbu port bottleneck (4-4.5M bpd vs 7M bpd pipeline inflow, 68% VLCC delays >36hr) is the BINDING constraint. Physical-futures gap ($132+ Dated vs $95 futures) prices port as binding, not pipeline. If Yanbu is bottleneck, physical-futures gap persists regardless of pipeline.

Day 52. IRGC permission regime now civilian-apex ENDORSED via SNSC statement April 18. Kinetic broadened Saturday: VLCC Jag Arnav fire + Sanmar Herald + container ship 'unknown projectile' (rocket/missile/drone) + Mein Schiff 4 VHF threat. Multi-type sweep (tanker + container + passenger) within 24 hours. IRGC operational control IS the structural output of the war. CENTCOM has not publicly corroborated Saturday kinetic 16-18 hours later — operational-source breadth is UKMTO + UK MoD single chain. SNSC + IRGC combined now speak with one institutional voice.

trigger_points.condition: Hormuz reopens to all traffic (even partially)
trigger_points.mechanism: Single biggest potential catalyst for oil price drop, India relief, rupee recovery, and equity market rebound. Would signal de-escalation. | UPDATE 2026-04-07: Bloomberg Hormuz Tracker confirms weekly transits at WAR HIGHS via Iran's three-tier system. THIS IS PARTIAL REOPENING in fact, though Iran calls it selective regulation rather than reopening. | UPDATE 2026-04-09: Iran RE-CLOSED Hormuz within 18 hours of ceasefire citing Israeli Lebanon strikes. Toggle mechanism confirmed — Iran can open/close at will keyed to Israeli compliance. Re-closure is NOT a new closure but a continuation of the controlled passage framework. Status remains WATCHING — the pattern is toggle (partial open/close), not sustained unconditional reopening.

trigger_points.condition: US attempts naval escort or forced opening of Hormuz
trigger_points.mechanism: APPROACHING ACTIVATION: US blockade operational since April 13. Day 1 results: deterrence working on shadow fleet (Ostria, Rich Starry turned back) but enforcement selective (Elpis transited without interception). UK-France announced separate multinational mission — 'strictly defensive,' could provide third pathway for commercial escorts. McNally still projects commercial escort operations possible by late April. HOWEVER: a blockade of Iranian ports is NOT the same as a naval escort service for commercial vessels. UK-France mission is the closest signal to an escort framework materializing. Watching for: (1) UK-France conference and coalition formation, (2) safe corridor shared with maritime industry, (3) VLCC/commercial vessel escort announced, (4) IRGC response to blockade enforcement. | UPDATE 2026-04-14: Status remains WATCHING. | UPDATE 2026-04-16: UK-France conference CONFIRMED Friday April 17 from Paris, 40+ countries — SCOPE NARROWED: France emphasizes escort mission 'would only take place when the war is over,' restoring navigation 'WHEN SECURITY CONDITIONS ALLOW.' This is POST-WAR framework, not immediate operational escort. Market's 'Friday produces operational escort' bull case removed. Status remains WATCHING — commitment to framework is signal, but no near-term insurance normalization catalyst.

trigger_points.condition: War risk insurance for Gulf transits breaks below 0.5% hull value (normalization threshold)
trigger_points.mechanism: Current 0.8-1.0% hull (for $100M VLCC = $800K-1M vs $150-250K pre-war). Insurers NOT stepping down on Trump interview or deal rhetoric — require SIGNED framework. Until war-risk breaks below 0.5%, market's hard-money pricing says Hormuz is NOT normalizing regardless of the talk. UK-France Friday post-war framework has no near-term insurance impact. Test: confirmed signed ceasefire + operational escort framework would drop premiums to ~0.3-0.4%. If insurance breaks below 0.5% within 30 days of Round 2, de-escalation trade verified; if stays above 0.8% after Round 2, Hormuz is structurally re-priced and oil floor is $90+.

trigger_points.condition: Hormuz remains under Iranian selective control even within ceasefire framework — ACTIVE 2026-04-08: Iran's 'controlled passage via coordination with armed forces' framing in the ceasefire text formalises the IRGC toll regime as the Iranian-side compliance mechanism. Even the bilateral US-Iran deal does NOT deliver unconditional Hormuz freedom. WSJ paradigm + Hormuz toll law + ceasefire 'controlled passage' language all converge.
trigger_points.mechanism: WSJ reports Trump willing to exit without Hormuz reopening. If war ends but Iran maintains toll system and selective passage, oil floor $90-100 permanently rather than reverting to $70. Changes the entire crisis-resolution investment thesis. | UPDATE 2026-04-07: Iran's 10-point counter formalizes 'safe-passage protocol' — i.e., permanent selective control under sovereignty claim. Trigger pre-conditions strengthening. | UPDATE 2026-04-18: DEFINITIVELY CONFIRMED. IRGC Navy issued formal permission regime April 17-18: civilian vessels only via Iran-designated routes, military vessels barred. Araghchi 'completely open' declaration is fenced by this regime + Baghaei conditional (US blockade must lift). Permission regime IS the operational codification of selective control. | UPDATE 2026-04-19: SNSC (civilian apex) FORMALLY RATIFIED IRGC closure posture + $1/bbl transit fee regime. Araghchi framework suspended until US lifts blockade. Priced chokepoint is now institutionally endorsed — Panama-Canal paradigm codified. Trigger remains ACTIVE; structural output is now institutional as well as operational.

trigger_points.condition: Physical-futures gap closes below $10/bbl (Dated Brent converges to Brent futures)
trigger_points.mechanism: Currently ~$40 (Dated Brent $132 / futures $91) — widest of crisis. Physical-futures convergence is the cleanest hard-money signal that Hormuz disruption is ACTUALLY resolving (not just deal rhetoric). | UPDATE 2026-04-19: Sunday SNSC ratification + kinetic broadening argue paper converges UPWARD to physical, not physical DOWN to paper. Monday Globex open (Sunday 22:00 GMT) is the test: if Brent gaps up 3%+ toward $95+, paper is beginning convergence; if Brent opens within 2% of $91.87, paper is pricing kinetic as one-off. Remains WATCHING — ~28 hours of Hormuz kinetic not yet processed by futures.

trigger_points.condition: Sustained kinetic action against multi-type vessels (tanker + non-tanker) continues for 72+ hours — escalation ladder from warning-shot to coercion
trigger_points.mechanism: Sadjadpour falsifiable test (Apr 18): 'if no further kinetic actions within 72 hours without US concession, Saturday was a warning shot.' Tightened Apr 19: SNSC statement + container + cruise broadening are INSTITUTIONAL codification, NOT additional kinetic — so test through Tuesday IST is on further gunboat fire, not statements. If another ship is fired on Sunday or Monday IST without US concession, ladder has stepped up and warning-shot-for-leverage thesis is weakening toward sustained coercion. Operational watch: UKMTO advisory count, CENTCOM corroboration status, Kpler transit counts. If cruise/passenger vessel is hit, automatic IMO-level emergency.

trigger_points.condition: Sustained kinetic absence Sunday-Tuesday IST + Lloyd's AWRP step UP — paper Brent tops $100 on insurance, not on kinetic
trigger_points.mechanism: Sunday confirmed no fresh fire; if AWRP republishes >=1.5% Monday at London open and Brent prints $100+ without renewed kinetic, the chokepoint instrument becomes a pure financial-sector mechanism. Tests Brew/Eurasia 'effective control' thesis distinct from kinetic ladder. Resolution window: Tuesday IST.

price_snapshots.trigger: Trump 'complete opening' vs Iran 'controlled passage' contradiction
price_snapshots.delta_context: Brent crashed -13.5% on the announcement; physical flows lag the political headline

price_snapshots.price: SNSC institutional codification + multi-type kinetic broadening
price_snapshots.trigger: Iran's civilian apex formalises Hormuz transit-certificate + $1/bbl fee regime; kinetic expands from tanker to container + passenger in 24h
price_snapshots.delta_context: First civilian-apex endorsement of IRGC hardline posture since March 20 Trump ultimatum. ICG: 'textbook escalation ladder — converts rhetorical tool into priced instrument.' Russia (TASS) in full endorsement of 'sovereign waters' framing; China factual; both veto-ready at UN. Physical-futures gap ~$40/bbl = crisis-widest at Friday close. Monday Globex (Sunday 22:00 GMT) is first re-pricing venue.

price_snapshots.price: Sunday Globex Brent ~$98 indicative; Lloyd's AWRP no Sunday republish
price_snapshots.trigger: Sunday non-event ceiling on Brent + UAE Al Jaber Sunday hardening + Araghchi insurance-market reframing
price_snapshots.delta_context: +8.4% Brent over Friday wire close. AWRP republish Monday London open is next hard tell; expected 1.2-1.8%. UAE single-chain hardening pending Monday corroboration.
